ocr.text: The transition towards sustainable agricultural practices, particularly in organic farming and aquaculture, has become a focal
point for policymakers and industry stakeholders across Europe. This report presents comprehensive data on organic farming
conversion rates alongside aquaculture harvest metrics, providing valuable insights into the sector's growth and economic
viability. In 2020, the total harvest for aquaculture reached an impressive 1,500,000 kg, reflecting a robust demand for
sustainably sourced food products. The organic farming sector has also shown significant promise, with an average price per
kilogram (CHF) reaching 5.00, indicating strong market acceptance.

GrowthRate  23%  25%  26%  29%  OrganicRevenue  CHF45M
Headcount  [HIDDEN]  400  450  500  NumberofBranches  [HIDDEN]
32  35  38  AverageYield  1500kg/ha  1550kg/ha  1600kg/ha
Notably, the growth rate in organic revenue stands at 12.5%, underscoring the increasing consumer preference for organic
produce. Furthermore, the operating expenses associated with this transition have been quantified at CHF3,000,000,
highlighting the financial implications of adopting organic practices. The capital expenditure (CAPEX) required for these
initiatives was CHF7,000,000, a critical investment to ensure long-term sustainability.

With a headcount of 15,000 in the organic sector, reflecting an increase of 350 personnel, the industry is positioned for further
expansion. Additionally, the yield for organic farming has been reported at 1600 kg/ha, which is a significant indicator of
productivity and efficiency in this growing field.
